verse_text: Thus says the Lord: 'A voice of lamentation, weeping, and mourning was heard in Ramah. Rachel does not wish to cease mourning deeply over her children, because they are no more.'

links.context: Jeremiah recorded the people of Jerusalem being led away to exile ([[JER.38:15]]). On their way to captivity, they passed Ramah, which was near Bethlehem, where Jacob's wife Rachel lay buried. In his prophecy, Jeremiah saw Rachel, even from the grave, moved with compassion for the fate that had befallen her descendants. Here Rachel is again weeping for her children, showing that the saints in heaven have awareness and compassion for those yet on earth. These slaughtered children are regarded as saints and martyrs in the Church and are known as the Holy Innocents. Just as Rachel was told that her children would return from exile in Babylon ([[JER.38:16]], [[JER.38:17]]), so Jesus will return from His exile in Egypt.
